meaning: n.侍从,(贵族家中规定仆人穿的衣服)制服,特定服装
examples: He was dressed in court livery.
Two servants came in rich livery and carried the Fir Tree into a large and splendid drawing-room.
The horses were post; and neither the carriage, nor the livery of the servant who preceded it, were familiar to them.
In Chicago, taxi and livery services companies are suing Uber for consumer fraud and other allegations.
Livery cab businesses in NYC have sought to stop the program's implementation via legal means.
